item.text: I’m inspecting the two allowed source files and the verification script first so I can make the contract change with the smallest edit set and keep `main` intact.

item.aggregated_output:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

workspace_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
log_file="$workspace_root/benchmark-verify.jsonl"
started_ms="$(node -e 'process.stdout.write(String(Date.now()))')"
set +e
output="$(bash "/home/akul_medexfinance_com/clasp/compiler/hosted/evals/contract-upgrade-suite/validate.sh" "lead-follow-up-window-upgrade" "$workspace_root" 2>&1)"
status=$?
set -e
ended_ms="$(node -e 'process.stdout.write(String(Date.now()))')"
printf '%s\n' "$output"
node -e 'const fs = require("fs"); fs.appendFileSync(process.argv[1], JSON.stringify({ startedAtMs: Number(process.argv[2]), endedAtMs: Number(process.argv[3]), exitCode: Number(process.argv[4]) }) + "\n");' "$log_file" "$started_ms" "$ended_ms" "$status"
exit "$status"

item.text: I have the current contract shape. Next I’m updating the shared records and the route/workflow wiring together so the new digest type propagates cleanly and `main` resolves to the escalation owner.

item.text: I’m editing the shared contract first, then I’ll switch the API and request builders over to the new digest type in `Main.clasp`.

The shared types are updated. I’m wiring those names and fields through the summarizer and downstream lookup request now, with a priority-based owner/window mapping so the High-priority path still lands on `"sales-manager"`.

item.text:
Updated the lead summary flow to use `LeadFollowUpDigest` end-to-end. The shared contract changes are in [Shared/Lead.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-follow-up-window-upgrade-oracle-raw-text-9igDE9/Shared/Lead.clasp), where the new digest schema replaces the old summary shape in the workflow state and the playbook lookup request now carries `followUpWindowDays` and `escalationOwner`.

In [Main.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-follow-up-window-upgrade-oracle-raw-text-9igDE9/Main.clasp), the summarizer and API route now return `LeadFollowUpDigest`, the downstream lookup request is built from the new fields, and `main` now evaluates to the High-priority escalation owner, `"sales-manager"`.

Verification passed with `bash scripts/verify.sh`.
